title: Wood Lantern
summary: A simple square dowel-frame lantern for a candle or battery light — three published sizes and three base-detail options, from jigsawed corner notches to stacked double bases.
description:
This lantern is a square frame of 3/4" dowels joined at the corners, built as two identical L-shaped assemblies that join into a complete box frame — get those two halves matching before joining them, since a lantern frame's whole visual appeal depends on genuinely square, even corners at this small scale.

The base offers a real choice of three finishing details, and the choice should be made before cutting: jigsaw notches out of the corners of a solid base board so it fits up inside the dowel legs flush, add small dowel feet under a base that sits below the legs instead, or stack two full bases for a heavier, more substantial-looking foundation. Each option uses the exact same frame above it — only the base treatment changes.

Build whichever size you need (small, medium, or large) from the same three-option base system; the dowel lengths scale up together, so choosing a size is a matter of picking one column in the cut list and following it through, not redesigning the lantern. A candle or battery LED puck light sits inside the finished frame — this is a display piece, not a sealed enclosure, so never leave an open flame inside it unattended.

tools.note: Only needed for the jigsawed-corner base option.

materials.name: 1x8 board, cut piece
materials.note: Medium size base; substitute 1x6 or 1x10 for small or large.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Small: 1x6 @ 5-1/2 in; large: 1x10 @ 9-1/4 in. Corners notched with a jigsaw for the flush-fit option.

steps.title: Build the two frame halves
steps.body: Attach two of the longer dowel uprights to one of the shorter cross pieces, forming an L-shaped assembly. Build two identical halves.

steps.title: Join the frame
steps.body: Attach the two L-shaped halves together with the remaining dowel cross pieces to form the complete box frame.

steps.title: Add the base
steps.body: For a flush-fit base, cut a 3/4" x 3/4" notch out of each corner of the base board with a jigsaw and attach inside the lantern legs with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. For a feet or stacked-base option, simply attach the base to the bottom of the frame with brad nails.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: For the feet or double-base options, add the small feet or second base with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails. Sand and finish. Add a candle or battery light — never leave an open flame unattended.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
